titel: Additional budget adjustments for 2025: Defence Materiel and further support for Ukraine.
html:
In this proposal, the government presents proposed changes to the state budget for 2025. These proposals are a consequence of the deteriorating security situation in Sweden's vicinity, due to Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

The proposal includes suggestions for increased authorizations to procure and develop, and subsequently donate, equipment and supplies that have been requested by Ukraine. The proposal also includes authorizations for the government to donate defense materiel that the Swedish Armed Forces can temporarily spare. Furthermore, the proposal suggests increased funding to prepare the materiel that is to be donated, as well as increased funding and authorizations to replace parts of the materiel that is proposed to be donated to Ukraine. Finally, the proposal includes suggestions for increased funding for support in the maritime sector, as well as increased funding and authorizations for support in civil defense.
tldr: The government intends to invest more money in defense and provide greater support to Ukraine due to the ongoing war. These funds will be used to purchase, repair, and donate equipment, as well as to strengthen Sweden's coastal defenses and security within communities.
lattlast:
The government has proposed changes to the 2025 budget. This is due to the increased instability in our region following Russia's full-scale war against Ukraine.

The proposal would grant the government greater authority to procure and develop equipment that Ukraine has requested. It also includes the ability to donate equipment that the Swedish Armed Forces does not need to use for a certain period. To make this possible, additional funds are needed to repair the equipment being donated and to purchase replacements for what is given away.

In addition to the support for Ukraine, the government is also proposing increased funding for the maritime sector, which includes shipping and water-related activities. This also involves providing more funding to the civilian defense sector, which encompasses the parts of society that protect us outside of the military defense system.
